{"parentUuid":"b4563ac6-300f-4fc0-a8ed-73bcca75e13b","isSidechain":false,"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /Users/danielcabral/root-repos/cm/test_collection_module/.claude/skills/build-from-spec\n\n# Build From Spec\n\nBuild a collection module from a provider spec, API doc, PDF, or URL.\n\n> Full SOP: `collection_module/docs/14-BUILD-FROM-SPEC.md`\n> Reference implementation: `collection_module/docs/STRIPE-REFERENCE.md`\n\n---\n\n## Step 1 — Get a spec\n\nIf the user provided a URL, PDF, or OpenAPI file, extract it:\n\n```bash\ncd collection_module && npm run extract:spec -- --input= --output=docs/provider-spec.md\n```\n\nIf the user already has a filled `docs/SPEC-TEMPLATE.md`, skip to Step 2.\n\nIf the user provided raw API documentation (pasted text, a webpage), read it and fill `docs/SPEC-TEMPLATE.md` manually. The required sections are:\n\n| Section | Required fields |\n|---|---|\n| Provider Overview | Name, API type (sdk/http), base URL or SDK package |\n| Authentication | Header name, header format |\n| Webhook Configuration | Signature header, signature algorithm |\n| Webhook Events | Event names + what Root Platform action to take |\n| Data Shapes | Payment object shape, customer object shape |\n| Status Mapping | Provider status → Root `PaymentStatus` |\n\n## Step 2 — Scaffold the provider\n\n```bash\ncd collection_module && npm run scaffold:provider -- --from-spec=docs/provider-spec.md --reason=\"<why>\"\n```\n\nOr with explicit flags:\n\n```bash\ncd collection_module && npm run scaffold:provider -- \\\n  --provider=<Name> \\\n  --api-type=<sdk|http> \\\n  --base-url=<url> \\\n  --auth-header=<header> \\\n  --webhook-header=<header> \\\n  --reason=\"<why>\"\n```\n\nShow the user the CLI output. **7 files** should be created:\n- `code/clients/{provider}-client.ts`\n- `code/services/{provider}.service.ts`\n- `code/adapters/{provider}-to-root-adapter.ts`\n- `code/interfaces/{provider}-events.ts`\n- `__tests__/clients/{provider}-client.test.ts`\n- `__tests__/services/{provider}.service.test.ts`\n- `__tests__/adapters/{provider}-to-root-adapter.test.ts`\n\n## Step 3 — Implement stubs\n\nRead each generated file and implement the `// TODO` sections. Use `docs/STRIPE-REFERENCE.md` as the pattern for every method.\n\n**Rules for all implementations:**\n- Wrap all API calls in `retryWithBackoff()`\n- Log at the start of every method: `this.logService.info('Creating customer', 'ProviderService', params)`\n- Throw `ModuleError` on failures, not raw errors\n- Use `LogService` only — no `console.log`\n\n### Service methods to implement\n\n| Method | What to implement |\n|---|---|\n| `createCustomer` | POST to provider's customer endpoint |\n| `createPaymentIntent` | POST to payment/charge endpoint |\n| `attachPaymentMethod` | Attach method to customer + set as default |\n| `cancelSubscription` | DELETE/cancel subscription |\n\n### Adapter — fill `statusMap`\n\nMap provider payment statuses to Root's `PaymentStatus`:\n\n```typescript\nconst statusMap: Record<string, string> = {\n  'provider_success_status': PaymentStatus.Successful,\n  'provider_pending_status': PaymentStatus.Pending,\n  'provider_failed_status':  PaymentStatus.Failed,\n  'provider_cancel_status':  PaymentStatus.Cancelled,\n};\n```\n\n### Events — replace placeholders with real event names\n\n```typescript\nexport const PROVIDER_EVENTS = {\n  PAYMENT_COMPLETED: 'actual.event.name',\n  PAYMENT_FAILED:    'actual.event.failed',\n} as const;\n```\n\n## Step 4 — Wire into the module\n\nFour files to update. Read each one before editing — the Stripe entries show the exact pattern.\n\n### 4a. DI container (`code/core/container.setup.ts`)\n\nRegister client and service under provider-agnostic tokens:\n\n```typescript\ncontainer.register(ServiceToken.PROVIDER_CLIENT, {\n  useClass: MyProviderClient,\n  lifetime: Lifetime.SINGLETON,\n});\ncontainer.register(ServiceToken.PROVIDER_SERVICE, {\n  useClass: MyProviderService,\n  lifetime: Lifetime.SINGLETON,\n});\n```\n\n### 4b. Webhooks (`code/webhook-hooks.ts`)\n\n1. Verify signature using `providerClient.verifyWebhookSignature()`\n2. Parse event body\n3. Add `case` entries for each event in `PROVIDER_EVENTS`\n\n### 4c. Lifecycle hooks (`code/lifecycle-hooks/`)\n\n| Hook file | What to call |\n|---|---|\n| `policy.hooks.ts` → `afterPolicyIssued` | `providerService.createCustomer(...)` then update `policy.app_data` |\n| `payment-method.hooks.ts` → `afterPolicyPaymentMethodAssigned` | `providerService.attachPaymentMethod(...)` |\n| `payment.hooks.ts` → `afterPaymentCreated` | `providerService.createPaymentIntent(...)` |\n\n### 4d. Config (`code/env.sample.ts`)\n\nAdd the provider's config keys (PROVIDER_SECRET_KEY_LIVE, PROVIDER_SECRET_KEY_TEST, PROVIDER_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET_LIVE, PROVIDER_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET_TEST, etc.)\n\n## Step 5 — Verify\n\n```bash\ncd collection_module && npm test\n```\n\nFix all failures. Then run `/review-implementation` for self-review.\n\n## Status Checklist\n\n```\n[ ] Spec filled (docs/SPEC-TEMPLATE.md or extracted)\n[ ] Scaffold run — 7 files created\n[ ] Service methods implemented (no TODO stubs remaining)\n[ ] Adapter statusMap filled\n[ ] Events constants use real event names\n[ ] DI container registrations added\n[ ] Webhooks: signature verification + event routing\n[ ] Lifecycle hooks: policy, payment, payment-method\n[ ] Config placeholders added to env.sample.ts\n[ ] All tests pass (npm test)\n[ ] Self-review complete (/review-implementation)\n```\n"}]},"isMeta":true,"uuid":"269cabcd-0667-4fce-9c40-2756f25f7bf3","timestamp":"2026-04-10T09:53:14.862Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/danielcabral/root-repos/cm/test_collection_module","sessionId":"0882c166-69dc-427f-976d-54dc4572c1e6","version":"2.1.100","gitBranch":"main"}

Read this before implementing any new provider — it shows every pattern in use.\n4\t\n5\t## Table of Contents\n6\t\n7\t| Section | What you'll find |\n8\t|---|---|\n9\t| [Client](#client) | SDK init, Root statuses |\n17\t\n18\t---\n19\t\n20\t## Client\n21\t\n22\t**File:** `code/clients/stripe-client.ts`\n23\t\n24\t```typescript\n25\timport Stripe from 'stripe';\n26\timport { getConfigService } from '../services/config-instance';\n27\timport { PaymentProviderClient, WebhookRequest } from '../interfaces/provider.interfaces';\n28\t\n29\texport default class StripeClient implements PaymentProviderClient {\n30\t  public readonly sdk: Stripe;\n31\t\n32\t  constructor() {\n33\t    const config = getConfigService();\n34\t    this.sdk = new Stripe(config.get('providerSecretKey'), {\n35\t      apiVersion: '2023-10-16',\n36\t    });\n37\t  }\n38\t\n39\t  verifyWebhookSignature(request: WebhookRequest, secret: string): boolean {\n40\t    try {\n41\t      this.sdk.webhooks.constructEvent(request.body, request.headers['stripe-signature'], secret);\n42\t      return true;\n43\t    } catch {\n44\t      return false;\n45\t    }\n46\t  }\n47\t}\n48\t```\n49\t\n50\tKey points:\n51\t- `providerSecretKey` resolves to `sk_test_xxx` (sandbox) or `sk_live_xxx` (production) via `ConfigurationService`\n52\t- `verifyWebhookSignature` uses `stripe.webhooks.constructEvent` — throws on invalid, so wrap in try/catch\n53\t- Expose the raw SDK as `sdk` — services call `stripeClient.sdk.customers.create(...)` directly\n54\t\n55\t---\n56\t\n57\t## Service\n58\t\n59\t**File:** `code/services/stripe.service.ts`\n60\t\n61\t### Create customer\n62\t\n63\t```typescript\n64\tasync createCustomer(params: CreateCustomerParams): Promise<ProviderCustomer> {\n65\t  this.logService.info('Creating customer', 'StripeService', params);\n66\t  try {\n67\t    const customer = await retryWithBackoff(() =>\n68\t      this.providerClient.sdk.customers.create({\n69\t        email: params.email,\n70\t        name: params.name,\n71\t        metadata: params.metadata,\n72\t      })\n73\t    );\n74\t    return {\n75\t      id: customer.id,\n76\t      email: customer.email,\n77\t      name: customer.name,\n78\t      metadata: customer.metadata,\n79\t    };\n80\t  } catch (err) {\n81\t    this.logService.error('Failed to create customer', 'StripeService', err);\n82\t    throw new ModuleError('Failed to create customer', { cause: err });\n83\t  }\n84\t}\n85\t```\n86\t\n87\t### Create payment intent\n88\t\n89\t```typescript\n90\tasync createPaymentIntent(params: CreatePaymentIntentParams): Promise<ProviderPaymentIntent> {\n91\t  this.logService.info('Creating payment intent', 'StripeService', params);\n92\t  const intent = await retryWithBackoff(() =>\n93\t    this.providerClient.sdk.paymentIntents.create({\n94\t      amount: params.amount,\n95\t      currency: params.currency,\n96\t      customer: params.customerId,\n97\t      payment_method: params.paymentMethodId,\n98\t      confirm: true,\n99\t      off_session: true,\n100\t      metadata: { root_payment_id: params.metadata?.rootPaymentId },\n101\t    })\n102\t  );\n103\t  return { id: intent.id, status: intent.status, amount: intent.amount, currency: intent.currency };\n104\t}\n105\t```\n106\t\n107\t### Attach payment method\n108\t\n109\t```typescript\n110\tasync attachPaymentMethod(params: AttachPaymentMethodParams): Promise<ProviderPaymentMethod> {\n111\t  const pm = await this.providerClient.sdk.paymentMethods.attach(params.paymentMethodId, {\n112\t    customer: params.customerId,\n113\t  });\n114\t  await this.providerClient.sdk.customers.update(params.customerId, {\n115\t    invoice_settings: { default_payment_method: params.paymentMethodId },\n116\t  });\n117\t  return { id: pm.id, type: pm.type };\n118\t}\n119\t```\n120\t\n121\t### Cancel subscription\n122\t\n123\t```typescript\n124\tasync cancelSubscription(subscriptionId: string): Promise<ProviderSubscription> {\n125\t  const sub = await this.providerClient.sdk.subscriptions.cancel(subscriptionId);\n126\t  return { id: sub.id, status: sub.status };\n127\t}\n128\t```\n129\t\n130\t---\n131\t\n132\t## Adapter\n133\t\n134\t**File:** `code/adapters/stripe-to-root-adapter.ts`\n135\t\n136\t### Invoice → Root payment update\n137\t\n138\t```typescript\n139\tconvertPaymentToRootUpdate(invoice: Stripe.Invoice, params: ConvertPaymentParams) {\n140\t  return {\n141\t    status: params.status,\n142\t    failure_reason: params.failureReason ?? invoice.last_finalization_error?.message,\n143\t    failure_action: FailureAction.BlockRetry,\n144\t  };\n145\t}\n146\t```\n147\t\n148\tUsed in: `invoice.paid` controller (pass `PaymentStatus.Successful`) and `invoice.payment_failed` controller (pass `PaymentStatus.Failed` + failure reason).\n149\t\n150\t### Customer → app_data\n151\t\n152\t```typescript\n153\tconvertCustomerToAppData(customer: Stripe.Customer): Record<string, any> {\n154\t  return {\n155\t    stripe_customer_id: customer.id,\n156\t    stripe_email: customer.email,\n157\t    stripe_default_payment_method: customer.invoice_settings?.default_payment_method,\n158\t    stripe_created_at: new Date(customer.created * 1000).toISOString(),\n159\t  };\n160\t}\n161\t```\n162\t\n163\tUsed in: `afterPolicyIssued` and `afterPolicyPaymentMethodAssigned` to store Stripe customer data on the Root policy.\n164\t\n165\t---\n166\t\n167\t## Events\n168\t\n169\t**File:** `code/interfaces/stripe-events.ts`\n170\t\n171\t```typescript\n172\texport const STRIPE_EVENTS = {\n173\t  INVOICE_PAID:                    'invoice.paid',\n174\t  INVOICE_PAYMENT_FAILED:          'invoice.payment_failed',\n175\t  INVOICE_CREATED:                 'invoice.created',\n176\t  PAYMENT_INTENT_SUCCEEDED:        'payment_intent.succeeded',\n177\t  PAYMENT_INTENT_FAILED:           'payment_intent.payment_failed',\n178\t  CHARGE_REFUNDED:                 'charge.refunded',\n179\t  CHARGE_DISPUTE_CREATED:          'charge.dispute.created',\n180\t  SUBSCRIPTION_SCHEDULE_UPDATED:   'subscription_schedule.updated',\n181\t} as const;\n182\t```\n183\t\n184\t---\n185\t\n186\t## Lifecycle Hooks\n187\t\n188\t**File:** `code/lifecycle-hooks/`\n189\t\n190\t### afterPolicyIssued\n191\t\n192\t```typescript\n193\texport async function afterPolicyIssued({ policy }): Promise<void> {\n194\t  const container = getContainer();\n195\t  const providerService = container.resolve<StripeService>(ServiceToken.PROVIDER_SERVICE);\n196\t  const rootClient = container.resolve(ServiceToken.ROOT_CLIENT);\n197\t  const adapter = new StripeToRootAdapter();\n198\t\n199\t  const customer = await providerService.createCustomer({\n200\t    email: policy.policyholder.email,\n201\t    name: `${policy.policyholder.first_name} ${policy.policyholder.last_name}`,\n202\t    metadata: { root_policy_id: policy.policy_id },\n203\t  });\n204\t\n205\t  await rootClient.SDK.updatePolicy({\n206\t    policyId: policy.policy_id,\n207\t    body: { app_data: adapter.convertCustomerToAppData(customer) },\n208\t  });\n209\t}\n210\t```\n211\t\n212\t### afterPolicyPaymentMethodAssigned\n213\t\n214\t```typescript\n215\texport async function afterPolicyPaymentMethodAssigned({ policy }): Promise<void> {\n216\t  const container = getContainer();\n217\t  const providerService = container.resolve<StripeService>(ServiceToken.PROVIDER_SERVICE);\n218\t  const rootClient = container.resolve(ServiceToken.ROOT_CLIENT);\n219\t\n220\t  const paymentMethod = await rootClient.SDK.getPolicyPaymentMethod({ policyId: policy.policy_id });\n221\t  const stripePaymentMethodId = paymentMethod.module.payment_method;\n222\t  const customerId = policy.app_data?.stripe_customer_id;\n223\t\n224\t  await providerService.attachPaymentMethod({ paymentMethodId: stripePaymentMethodId, customerId });\n225\t}\n226\t```\n227\t\n228\t### Verify signature\n259\t  const isValid = providerClient.verifyWebhookSignature(\n260\t    { body: event.body, headers: event.headers },\n261\t    config.get('providerWebhookSigningSecret'),\n262\t  );\n263\t  if (!isValid) {\n264\t    logService.warn('Webhook signature invalid', 'WebhookHandler');\n265\t    return { statusCode: 400, body: JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid signature' }) };\n266\t  }\n267\t\n268\t  // 2. Parse event\n269\t  const parsed = JSON.parse(event.body);\n270\t  logService.info(`Webhook received: ${parsed.type}`, 'WebhookHandler', { id: parsed.id });\n271\t\n272\t  // 3. Route\n273\t  switch (parsed.type) {\n274\t    case STRIPE_EVENTS.INVOICE_PAID: {\n275\t      const ctrl = container.resolve(ServiceToken.INVOICE_PAID_CONTROLLER);\n276\t      await ctrl.handle(parsed.data.object);\n277\t      break;\n278\t    }\n279\t    case STRIPE_EVENTS.INVOICE_PAYMENT_FAILED: {\n280\t      const ctrl = container.resolve(ServiceToken.INVOICE_PAYMENT_FAILED_CONTROLLER);\n281\t      await ctrl.handle(parsed.data.object);\n282\t      break;\n283\t    }\n284\t    default:\n285\t      logService.info(`Unhandled event type: ${parsed.type}`, 'WebhookHandler');\n286\t  }\n287\t\n288\t  return { statusCode: 200, body: JSON.stringify({ received: true }) };\n289\t}\n290\t```\n291\t\n292\t---\n293\t\n294\t## Config Fields\n295\t\n296\tFields used by the Stripe implementation in `code/env.ts` / `code/env.sample.ts`:\n297\t\n298\t| Config key (via Stripe Reference Implementation\n\n> The working example. Read this before implementing any new provider — it shows every pattern in use.\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n| Section | What you'll find |\n|---|---|\n| [Client](#client) | SDK init, webhook verification |\n| [Service](#service) | Customer, payment intent, payment method, subscription |\n| [Adapter](#adapter) | Invoice → Root payment, customer → app_data |\n| [Events](#events) | Webhook event constants |\n| [Lifecycle Hooks](#lifecycle-hooks) | afterPolicyIssued, afterPolicyPaymentMethodAssigned, afterPaymentCreated |\n| [Webhook Routing](#webhook-routing) | Signature verify + switch statement |\n| [Config Fields](#config-fields) | env.ts keys used by Stripe |\n| [Status Mapping](#status-mapping) | Stripe statuses → Root statuses |\n\n---\n\n## Client\n\n**File:** `code/clients/stripe-client.ts`\n\n```typescript\nimport Stripe from 'stripe';\nimport { getConfigService } from '../services/config-instance';\nimport { PaymentProviderClient, WebhookRequest } from '../interfaces/provider.interfaces';\n\nexport default class StripeClient implements PaymentProviderClient {\n  public readonly sdk: Stripe;\n\n  constructor() {\n    const config = getConfigService();\n    this.sdk = new Stripe(config.get('providerSecretKey'), {\n      apiVersion: '2023-10-16',\n    });\n  }\n\n  verifyWebhookSignature(request: WebhookRequest, secret: string): boolean {\n    try {\n      this.sdk.webhooks.constructEvent(request.body, request.headers['stripe-signature'], secret);\n      return true;\n    } catch {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nKey points:\n- `providerSecretKey` resolves to `sk_test_xxx` (sandbox) or `sk_live_xxx` (production) via `ConfigurationService`\n- `verifyWebhookSignature` uses `stripe.webhooks.constructEvent` — throws on invalid, so wrap in try/catch\n- Expose the raw SDK as `sdk` — services call `stripeClient.sdk.customers.create(...)` directly\n\n---\n\n## Service\n\n**File:** `code/services/stripe.service.ts`\n\n### Create customer\n\n```typescript\nasync createCustomer(params: CreateCustomerParams): Promise<ProviderCustomer> {\n  this.logService.info('Creating customer', 'StripeService', params);\n  try {\n    const customer = await retryWithBackoff(() =>\n      this.providerClient.sdk.customers.create({\n        email: params.email,\n        name: params.name,\n        metadata: params.metadata,\n      })\n    );\n    return {\n      id: customer.id,\n      email: customer.email,\n      name: customer.name,\n      metadata: customer.metadata,\n    };\n  } catch (err) {\n    this.logService.error('Failed to create customer', 'StripeService', err);\n    throw new ModuleError('Failed to create customer', { cause: err });\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Create payment intent\n\n```typescript\nasync createPaymentIntent(params: CreatePaymentIntentParams): Promise<ProviderPaymentIntent> {\n  this.logService.info('Creating payment intent', 'StripeService', params);\n  const intent = await retryWithBackoff(() =>\n    this.providerClient.sdk.paymentIntents.create({\n      amount: params.amount,\n      currency: params.currency,\n      customer: params.customerId,\n      payment_method: params.paymentMethodId,\n      confirm: true,\n      off_session: true,\n      metadata: { root_payment_id: params.metadata?.rootPaymentId },\n    })\n  );\n  return { id: intent.id, status: intent.status, amount: intent.amount, currency: intent.currency };\n}\n```\n\n### Attach payment method\n\n```typescript\nasync attachPaymentMethod(params: AttachPaymentMethodParams): Promise<ProviderPaymentMethod> {\n  const pm = await this.providerClient.sdk.paymentMethods.attach(params.paymentMethodId, {\n    customer: params.customerId,\n  });\n  await this.providerClient.sdk.customers.update(params.customerId, {\n    invoice_settings: { default_payment_method: params.paymentMethodId },\n  });\n  return { id: pm.id, type: pm.type };\n}\n```\n\n### Cancel subscription\n\n```typescript\nasync cancelSubscription(subscriptionId: string): Promise<ProviderSubscription> {\n  const sub = await this.providerClient.sdk.subscriptions.cancel(subscriptionId);\n  return { id: sub.id, status: sub.status };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Adapter\n\n**File:** `code/adapters/stripe-to-root-adapter.ts`\n\n### Invoice → Root payment update\n\n```typescript\nconvertPaymentToRootUpdate(invoice: Stripe.Invoice, params: ConvertPaymentParams) {\n  return {\n    status: params.status,\n    failure_reason: params.failureReason ?? invoice.last_finalization_error?.message,\n    failure_action: FailureAction.BlockRetry,\n  };\n}\n```\n\nUsed in: `invoice.paid` controller (pass `PaymentStatus.Successful`) and `invoice.payment_failed` controller (pass `PaymentStatus.Failed` + failure reason).\n\n### Customer → app_data\n\n```typescript\nconvertCustomerToAppData(customer: Stripe.Customer): Record<string, any> {\n  return {\n    stripe_customer_id: customer.id,\n    stripe_email: customer.email,\n    stripe_default_payment_method: customer.invoice_settings?.default_payment_method,\n    stripe_created_at: new Date(customer.created * 1000).toISOString(),\n  };\n}\n```\n\nUsed in: `afterPolicyIssued` and `afterPolicyPaymentMethodAssigned` to store Stripe customer data on the Root policy.\n\n---\n\n## Events\n\n**File:** `code/interfaces/stripe-events.ts`\n\n```typescript\nexport const STRIPE_EVENTS = {\n  INVOICE_PAID:                    'invoice.paid',\n  INVOICE_PAYMENT_FAILED:          'invoice.payment_failed',\n  INVOICE_CREATED:                 'invoice.created',\n  PAYMENT_INTENT_SUCCEEDED:        'payment_intent.succeeded',\n  PAYMENT_INTENT_FAILED:           'payment_intent.payment_failed',\n  CHARGE_REFUNDED:                 'charge.refunded',\n  CHARGE_DISPUTE_CREATED:          'charge.dispute.created',\n  SUBSCRIPTION_SCHEDULE_UPDATED:   'subscription_schedule.updated',\n} as const;\n```\n\n---\n\n## Lifecycle Hooks\n\n**File:** `code/lifecycle-hooks/`\n\n### afterPolicyIssued\n\n```typescript\nexport async function afterPolicyIssued({ policy }): Promise<void> {\n  const container = getContainer();\n  const providerService = container.resolve<StripeService>(ServiceToken.PROVIDER_SERVICE);\n  const rootClient = container.resolve(ServiceToken.ROOT_CLIENT);\n  const adapter = new StripeToRootAdapter();\n\n  const customer = await providerService.createCustomer({\n    email: policy.policyholder.email,\n    name: `${policy.policyholder.first_name} ${policy.policyholder.last_name}`,\n    metadata: { root_policy_id: policy.policy_id },\n  });\n\n  await rootClient.SDK.updatePolicy({\n    policyId: policy.policy_id,\n    body: { app_data: adapter.convertCustomerToAppData(customer) },\n  });\n}\n```\n\n### afterPolicyPaymentMethodAssigned\n\n```typescript\nexport async function afterPolicyPaymentMethodAssigned({ policy }): Promise<void> {\n  const container = getContainer();\n  const providerService = container.resolve<StripeService>(ServiceToken.PROVIDER_SERVICE);\n  const rootClient = container.resolve(ServiceToken.ROOT_CLIENT);\n\n  const paymentMethod = await rootClient.SDK.getPolicyPaymentMethod({ policyId: policy.policy_id });\n  const stripePaymentMethodId = paymentMethod.module.payment_method;\n  const customerId = policy.app_data?.stripe_customer_id;\n\n  await providerService.attachPaymentMethod({ paymentMethodId: stripePaymentMethodId, customerId });\n}\n```\n\n### afterPaymentCreated\n\n```typescript\nexport async function afterPaymentCreated({ policy, payment }): Promise<void> {\n  const container = getContainer();\n  const providerService = container.resolve<StripeService>(ServiceToken.PROVIDER_SERVICE);\n\n  await providerService.createPaymentIntent({\n    amount: payment.amount,\n    currency: policy.currency ?? 'ZAR',\n    customerId: policy.app_data.stripe_customer_id,\n    paymentMethodId: policy.app_data.stripe_payment_method_id,\n    metadata: { rootPaymentId: payment.payment_id, rootPolicyId: policy.policy_id },\n  });\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Webhook Routing\n\n**File:** `code/webhook-hooks.ts`\n\n```typescript\nexport async function processWebhookRequest(event: any): Promise<any> {\n  const container = getContainer();\n  const config = container.resolve<ConfigurationService>(ServiceToken.CONFIG_SERVICE);\n  const providerClient = container.resolve<StripeClient>(ServiceToken.PROVIDER_CLIENT);\n  const logService = container.resolve<LogService>(ServiceToken.LOG_SERVICE);\n\n  // 1. Verify signature\n  const isValid = providerClient.verifyWebhookSignature(\n    { body: event.body, headers: event.headers },\n    config.get('providerWebhookSigningSecret'),\n  );\n  if (!isValid) {\n    logService.warn('Webhook signature invalid', 'WebhookHandler');\n    return { statusCode: 400, body: JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid signature' }) };\n  }\n\n  // 2. Parse event\n  const parsed = JSON.parse(event.body);\n  logService.info(`Webhook received: ${parsed.type}`, 'WebhookHandler', { id: parsed.id });\n\n  // 3. Route\n  switch (parsed.type) {\n    case STRIPE_EVENTS.INVOICE_PAID: {\n      const ctrl = container.resolve(ServiceToken.INVOICE_PAID_CONTROLLER);\n      await ctrl.handle(parsed.data.object);\n      break;\n    }\n    case STRIPE_EVENTS.INVOICE_PAYMENT_FAILED: {\n      const ctrl = container.resolve(ServiceToken.INVOICE_PAYMENT_FAILED_CONTROLLER);\n      await ctrl.handle(parsed.data.object);\n      break;\n    }\n    default:\n      logService.info(`Unhandled event type: ${parsed.type}`, 'WebhookHandler');\n  }\n\n  return { statusCode: 200, body: JSON.stringify({ received: true }) };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Config Fields\n\nFields used by the Stripe implementation in `code/env.ts` / `code/env.sample.ts`:\n\n| Config key (via `config.get(...)`) | env.ts export | Example value Here is the comprehensive structured summary:\n\n---\n\n## Adyen API Documentation -- Comprehensive Integration Reference\n\n### 1. Authentication\n\n**API Key Authentication (recommended)**\n- **Header**: `X-API-Key`\n- **Value**: API key generated from the Adyen Customer Area\n- **Scope**: Each API credential has role-based permissions\n\n**Basic Authentication (legacy)**\n- Username format: `ws_123456@Company.[YourCompanyAccount]`\n- Password: generated in Customer Area\n\n**Client Key**: Used for client-side (Drop-in/Components) -- not used server-side\n\n**Key Rotation**: When you generate a new API key, the previous key remains active for 24 hours.\n\n---\n\n### 2. Base URLs\n\n| Environment | URL |\n|---|---|\n| **Test** | `https://checkout-test.adyen.com/v71` |\n| **Live** | `https://{PREFIX}-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout/v71` |\n| **Recurring (legacy, test)** | `https://pal-test.adyen.com/pal/servlet/Recurring/v68` |\n| **Recurring (legacy, live)** | `https://{PREFIX}-paltokenization-live.adyenpayments.com/paltokenization/servlet/Recurring/v68` |\n\nThe `{PREFIX}` is a unique live URL prefix generated in the Customer Area.\n\n---\n\n### 3. Checkout / Payments API Endpoints\n\n#### POST `/payments` -- Start a transaction\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required) -- your merchant account name\n- `amount` (object, required) -- `{ currency: \"EUR\", value: 1000 }` (value in minor units, i.e. cents)\n- `reference` (string, required) -- your unique payment reference\n- `paymentMethod` (object, required) -- varies by method (e.g. `{ type: \"scheme\", number: \"...\", expiryMonth: \"...\", expiryYear: \"...\", cvc: \"...\" }`)\n- `returnUrl` (string) -- URL to redirect after 3DS/redirects\n- `shopperReference` (string) -- unique ID for the shopper (required for tokenization)\n- `shopperInteraction` (enum) -- `Ecommerce`, `ContAuth`, `Moto`, `POS`\n- `recurringProcessingModel` (enum) -- `CardOnFile`, `Subscription`, `UnscheduledCardOnFile`\n- `storePaymentMethod` (boolean) -- `true` to save payment details\n- `storedPaymentMethodId` (string) -- token ID for charging a saved method\n- `channel` (string) -- `Web`, `iOS`, `Android`\n- `countryCode` (string) -- two-letter country code\n- `additionalData` (object) -- extra data fields\n\n**Response fields:**\n- `resultCode` (string) -- see result codes below\n- `pspReference` (string) -- Adyen unique reference for the payment\n- `action` (object) -- present if redirect/3DS challenge needed\n- `additionalData` (object) -- extra response data\n\n**HTTP responses:** 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 422 Unprocessable Entity, 500 Server Error\n\n#### POST `/sessions` -- Create a payment session (simplified flow)\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `amount` (object, required)\n- `reference` (string, required)\n- `returnUrl` (string, required)\n- `shopperReference` (string)\n- `storePaymentMethod` (boolean)\n- `recurringProcessingModel` (string)\n- `expiresAt` (string) -- session expiry time\n- `countryCode` (string)\n\n**Response fields:**\n- `id` (string) -- session ID\n- `sessionData` (string) -- encrypted session data for client-side\n- Additional payment configuration\n\n#### POST `/paymentMethods` -- List available payment methods\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `amount` (object)\n- `countryCode` (string)\n- `shopperLocale` (string)\n- `shopperReference` (string) -- include to get stored payment methods\n\n**Response fields:**\n- `paymentMethods` (array) -- available payment methods\n- `storedPaymentMethods` (array) -- saved payment methods for the shopper\n\n#### POST `/payments/details` -- Submit additional payment details (3DS, redirects)\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `details` (object) -- details from the redirect/challenge\n- `paymentData` (string) -- from the original payment response action\n\n**Response fields:**\n- `resultCode` (string)\n- `pspReference` (string)\n- `action` (object) -- if further action needed\n- `additionalData` (object)\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/captures` -- Capture an authorized payment\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `amount` (object, required) -- can be partial\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `reference` (string) -- your capture reference\n\n**Response (201):**\n- `pspReference` (string) -- capture operation reference\n- `paymentPspReference` (string) -- original payment reference\n- `status` (string) -- `\"received\"`\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/cancels` -- Cancel an authorized payment\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `reference` (string)\n\n**Response (201):**\n- `pspReference` (string)\n- `paymentPspReference` (string)\n- `status` (string) -- `\"received\"`\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/refunds` -- Refund a captured payment\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `amount` (object, required) -- can be partial\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `reference` (string)\n- `merchantRefundReason` (string) -- `FRAUD`, `CUSTOMER REQUEST`, `RETURN`, `DUPLICATE`, `OTHER`\n\n**Response (201):**\n- `pspReference` (string)\n- `paymentPspReference` (string)\n- `status` (string) -- `\"received\"`\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/reversals` -- Reversal (auto cancel or refund)\n\nAutomatically determines whether to cancel (if uncaptured) or refund (if captured). Full amount only.\n\n**Response (201):**\n- `pspReference` (string)\n- `paymentPspReference` (string)\n- `status` (string) -- `\"received\"`\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/amountUpdates` -- Adjust authorized amount\n\n#### POST `/cancels` -- Cancel by merchant reference (within 24h, no PSP reference needed)\n\n---\n\n### 4. Customer/Shopper Management\n\nAdyen does NOT have a separate \"Customer\" resource like Stripe. Instead, shoppers are identified by `shopperReference`:\n\n- **shopperReference**: Your unique identifier for the shopper (minimum 3 characters, no PII like email/name)\n- Used consistently across all requests to link payments, tokens, and stored methods to a shopper\n- Token Groups feature (on request) can share references across multiple merchant accounts\n\n**Shopper fields in payment requests:**\n- `shopperReference` (string) -- your unique shopper ID\n- `shopperEmail` (string)\n- `shopperName` (object) -- `{ firstName, lastName }`\n- `shopperIP` (string)\n- `billingAddress` (object)\n- `deliveryAddress` (object)\n- `dateOfBirth` (string)\n- `telephoneNumber` (string)\n- `shopperLocale` (string)\n\n---\n\n### 5. Payment Methods / Tokenization\n\n#### Creating a Token (saving a payment method)\n\nInclude in the `/payments` request:\n```json\n{\n  \"shopperReference\": \"YOUR_SHOPPER_ID\",\n  \"storePaymentMethod\": true,\n  \"shopperInteraction\": \"Ecommerce\",\n  \"recurringProcessingModel\": \"CardOnFile\",\n  \"merchantAccount\": \"YOUR_MERCHANT_ACCOUNT\",\n  \"amount\": { \"currency\": \"EUR\", \"value\": 1000 },\n  \"reference\": \"YOUR_REFERENCE\",\n  \"paymentMethod\": { \"type\": \"scheme\", \"encryptedCardNumber\": \"...\", \"encryptedExpiryMonth\": \"...\", \"encryptedExpiryYear\": \"...\", \"encryptedSecurityCode\": \"...\" }\n}\n```\n\nToken is delivered via `recurring.token.created` webhook with `data.storedPaymentMethodId`.\n\n#### Listing Stored Payment Methods\n\n**GET `/storedPaymentMethods?merchantAccount=X&shopperReference=Y`**\n\nResponse `storedPaymentMethods` array contains:\n- `id` -- the token ID (storedPaymentMethodId)\n- `supportedRecurringProcessingModels` -- `[\"CardOnFile\"]`, `[\"Subscription\"]`, etc.\n- Card details (masked), brand, expiry, etc.\n\n#### Deleting a Token\n\n**DELETE `/storedPaymentMethods/{storedPaymentMethodId}?merchantAccount=X&shopperReference=Y`**\n\n#### Legacy Recurring API (v68)\n\n- **POST `/listRecurringDetails`** -- list tokens (legacy)\n- **POST `/disable`** -- disable/delete tokens (legacy)\n\nAdyen recommends using Checkout API endpoints instead.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Recurring / Subscriptions\n\nAdyen supports three recurring models:\n\n| Model | shopperInteraction | recurringProcessingModel | Use Case |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **Card on File** | `Ecommerce` (first), `ContAuth` (subsequent) | `CardOnFile` | One-click checkout, returning customers |\n| **Subscription** | `Ecommerce` (first), `ContAuth` (subsequent) | `Subscription` | Fixed-schedule recurring charges |\n| **Unscheduled Card on File** | `Ecommerce` (first), `ContAuth` (subsequent) | `UnscheduledCardOnFile` | Variable-schedule merchant-initiated charges |\n\n#### First Payment (create token)\n```json\n{\n  \"amount\": { \"currency\": \"USD\", \"value\": 1000 },\n  \"merchantAccount\": \"YOUR_MERCHANT_ACCOUNT\",\n  \"reference\": \"FIRST_PAYMENT_REF\",\n  \"shopperReference\": \"SHOPPER_123\",\n  \"storePaymentMethod\": true,\n  \"shopperInteraction\": \"Ecommerce\",\n  \"recurringProcessingModel\": \"Subscription\",\n  \"paymentMethod\": { \"type\": \"scheme\", ... },\n  \"returnUrl\": \"https://your-site.com/return\"\n}\n```\n\n#### Subsequent Payment (charge saved token)\n```json\n{\n  \"amount\": { \"currency\": \"USD\", \"value\": 1000 },\n  \"merchantAccount\": \"YOUR_MERCHANT_ACCOUNT\",\n  \"reference\": \"RECURRING_PAYMENT_REF\",\n  \"shopperReference\": \"SHOPPER_123\",\n  \"shopperInteraction\": \"ContAuth\",\n  \"recurringProcessingModel\": \"Subscription\",\n  \"paymentMethod\": {\n    \"type\": \"scheme\",\n    \"storedPaymentMethodId\": \"TOKEN_ID_FROM_WEBHOOK\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\nNote: `returnUrl` not needed for `ContAuth` merchant-initiated payments. The `/sessions` endpoint cannot be used for merchant-initiated subsequent payments -- you must use `/payments`.\n\n---\n\n### 7. Webhooks\n\n#### Configuration\n\nWebhooks are configured in the Customer Area or via the Management API. You must respond with HTTP `2xx` status code and `[accepted]` in the response body for standard webhooks.\n\n#### Standard Webhook Event Codes (Payment-related)\n\n| Event Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `AUTHORISATION` | Payment authorization result (success/failure). Primary event for payment outcomes. |\n| `CAPTURE` | Capture request result. Not sent for automatic captures by default. |\n| `CAPTURE_FAILED` | Capture rejected by card scheme (rare). |\n| `CANCELLATION` | Cancellation request result. |\n| `CANCEL_OR_REFUND` | Result of cancel-or-refund (reversal) request. |\n| `REFUND` | Refund validation succeeded; sent to card scheme. |\n| `REFUND_FAILED` | Refund rejected by card scheme. |\n| `REFUNDED_REVERSED` | Refunded amount returned to merchant (e.g. invalid bank account). |\n| `REFUND_WITH_DATA` | Outcome of unreferenced refund. |\n| `EXPIRE` | Payment expired on Adyen platform. |\n| `HANDLED_EXTERNALLY` | Payment handled outside Adyen. |\n| `ORDER_OPENED` | First partial payment created an order. |\n| `ORDER_CLOSED` | Final outcome for partial payment orders. |\n| `VOID_PENDING_REFUND` | Cancellation of unreferenced POS refund. |\n| `RECURRING_CONTRACT` | Recurring contract/token created (non-default, must enable). |\n\n#### Dispute Event Codes\n\n| Event Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `CHARGEBACK` | Funds deducted due to chargeback. |\n| `CHARGEBACK_REVERSED` | Chargeback defense initially successful (not final). |\n| `NOTIFICATION_OF_CHARGEBACK` | Dispute opened; investigate and respond. |\n| `NOTIFICATION_OF_FRAUD` | Issuer fraud alert (Visa TC40/Mastercard SAFE). |\n| `REQUEST_FOR_INFORMATION` | Customer opened RFI case. |\n| `SECOND_CHARGEBACK` | Original chargeback defense declined. |\n| `PREARBITRATION_LOST` / `PREARBITRATION_WON` | Pre-arbitration outcomes. |\n\n#### Token Lifecycle Webhook Events\n\n| Event | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `recurring.token.created` | New token created. Contains `data.storedPaymentMethodId`. |\n| `recurring.token.updated` | Token details modified. |\n| `recurring.token.disabled` | Token removed or disabled. |\n| `recurring.token.alreadyExisting` | Duplicate token attempt. |\n\n#### Standard Webhook Payload Structure\n\n```json\n{\n  \"live\": \"false\",\n  \"notificationItems\": [\n    {\n      \"NotificationRequestItem\": {\n        \"eventCode\": \"AUTHORISATION\",\n        \"success\": \"true\",\n        \"pspReference\": \"7914073381342284\",\n        \"originalReference\": \"\",\n        \"merchantAccountCode\": \"YOUR_MERCHANT_ACCOUNT\",\n        \"merchantReference\": \"YOUR_PAYMENT_REFERENCE\",\n        \"amount\": {\n          \"currency\": \"EUR\",\n          \"value\": 1130\n        },\n        \"eventDate\": \"2024-01-15T10:30:00+01:00\",\n        \"operations\": [\"CANCEL\", \"CAPTURE\", \"REFUND\"],\n        \"paymentMethod\": \"visa\",\n        \"reason\": \"\",\n        \"additionalData\": {\n          \"hmacSignature\": \"BASE64_ENCODED_SIGNATURE\"\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n**Required response**: HTTP 200 with body `[accepted]`\n\n#### HMAC Signature Verification\n\n**Algorithm**: HMAC-SHA256\n\n**Fields concatenated in order** (colon-delimited):\n1. `pspReference`\n2. `originalReference`\n3. `merchantAccountCode`\n4. `merchantReference`\n5. `amount.value`\n6. `amount.currency`\n7. `eventCode`\n8. `success`\n\n**Example concatenation**:\n```\n7914073381342284::TestMerchant:TestPayment-1407325143704:1130:EUR:AUTHORISATION:true\n```\n\n**Calculation steps**:\n1. Concatenate fields with `:` delimiter (empty string for missing fields)\n2. Convert payload string to binary (UTF-8)\n3. Convert HMAC key from hex string to binary\n4. Compute HMAC-SHA256(binary_key, binary_payload)\n5. Base64-encode the result\n6. Compare with `additionalData.hmacSignature`\n\n**HMAC key**: Hex string generated in Customer Area (e.g. `44782DEF547AAA06C910C43932B1EB0C71FC68D9D0C057550C48EC2ACF6BA056`)\n\n**Node.js reference implementation**: [adyen-node-api-library hmacValidator.ts](https://github.com/Adyen/adyen-node-api-library/blob/7e76d1b4c87d334f98a1ddc70b4e30a225244d2e/src/utils/hmacValidator.ts)\n\n---\n\n### 8. Payment Result Codes (Complete List)\n\n#### Final States\n\n| Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `Authorised` | Payment successfully authorized. Proceed with delivery. |\n| `Refused` | Payment refused by issuer/acquirer. Check `refusalReason` and `refusalReasonCode`. |\n| `Cancelled` | Payment cancelled by shopper or merchant. |\n| `Error` | Error during processing. |\n\n#### Intermediate States\n\n| Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `Received` | Payment received by Adyen, will be processed (async methods like SEPA). |\n| `Pending` | Payment received, final status not yet available. |\n| `PresentToShopper` | Additional info must be shown to shopper (vouchers, QR codes). |\n\n#### Action-Required States (3DS / Redirects)\n\n| Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `RedirectShopper` | Shopper must be redirected to external page (3DS1, bank redirect). |\n| `ChallengeShopper` | 3DS2 challenge required from issuer. |\n| `IdentifyShopper` | 3DS2 device fingerprinting required. |\n\n#### Special States\n\n| Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `AuthenticationFinished` | 3DS2 authentication completed (authentication-only flow). |\n| `AuthenticationNotRequired` | 3DS not required for this transaction. |\n| `PartiallyAuthorised` | Only part of the amount was authorized. |\n\n#### Refusal Reason Codes (when resultCode = Refused)\n\nKey codes: `2` (Refused), `5` (Blocked Card), `6` (Expired Card), `8` (Invalid Card Number), `12` (Not Enough Balance), `14` (Acquirer Fraud), `20` (FRAUD), `24` (CVC Declined), `31` (Issuer Suspected Fraud), `38` (Authentication Required -- retry with 3DS).\n\n---\n\n### 9. Idempotency\n\nSupported via `Idempotency-Key` header on requests. Use a unique key per operation to safely retry failed requests.\n\n---\n\n### 10. Key Integration Notes\n\n- **Amount format**: Always in minor units (cents). EUR 10.00 = `{ currency: \"EUR\", value: 1000 }`\n- **Captures, cancels, refunds are asynchronous**: The API returns `status: \"received\"` immediately. The actual outcome arrives via webhook.\n- **Rely on webhooks, not API responses**: Adyen explicitly recommends relying on the `AUTHORISATION` webhook rather than the `/payments` response for the definitive payment result.\n- **Automatic vs manual capture**: Default is automatic (immediate capture). Can be set to manual or delayed in Customer Area or per-request.\n- **Partial captures**: Supported; unclaimed amount is auto-cancelled (single) or held (multiple, requires enablement).\n- **Partial refunds**: Supported; cumulative total must not exceed captured amount.\n- **No separate Customer API**: Use `shopperReference` consistently across all calls. No need to \"create\" a customer first."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a6fef895e50d4b1de (use SendMessage with to: 'a6fef895e50d4b1de' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 38291\ntool_uses: 37\nduration_ms: 407958</usage>"}]}]},"uuid":"8f9c6ff7-b714-4a53-9028-95c6eae4996c","timestamp":"2026-04-10T10:01:37.317Z","toolUseResult":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Fetch the Adyen API documentation from https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/ and extract all relevant information needed to build a payment provider integration. Here is the comprehensive structured summary:\n\n---\n\n## Adyen API Documentation -- Comprehensive Integration Reference\n\n### 1. Authentication\n\n**API Key Authentication (recommended)**\n- **Header**: `X-API-Key`\n- **Value**: API key generated from the Adyen Customer Area\n- **Scope**: Each API credential has role-based permissions\n\n**Basic Authentication (legacy)**\n- Username format: `ws_123456@Company.[YourCompanyAccount]`\n- Password: generated in Customer Area\n\n**Client Key**: Used for client-side (Drop-in/Components) -- not used server-side\n\n**Key Rotation**: When you generate a new API key, the previous key remains active for 24 hours.\n\n---\n\n### 2. Base URLs\n\n| Environment | URL |\n|---|---|\n| **Test** | `https://checkout-test.adyen.com/v71` |\n| **Live** | `https://{PREFIX}-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout/v71` |\n| **Recurring (legacy, test)** | `https://pal-test.adyen.com/pal/servlet/Recurring/v68` |\n| **Recurring (legacy, live)** | `https://{PREFIX}-paltokenization-live.adyenpayments.com/paltokenization/servlet/Recurring/v68` |\n\nThe `{PREFIX}` is a unique live URL prefix generated in the Customer Area.\n\n---\n\n### 3. Checkout / Payments API Endpoints\n\n#### POST `/payments` -- Start a transaction\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required) -- your merchant account name\n- `amount` (object, required) -- `{ currency: \"EUR\", value: 1000 }` (value in minor units, i.e. cents)\n- `reference` (string, required) -- your unique payment reference\n- `paymentMethod` (object, required) -- varies by method (e.g. `{ type: \"scheme\", number: \"...\", expiryMonth: \"...\", expiryYear: \"...\", cvc: \"...\" }`)\n- `returnUrl` (string) -- URL to redirect after 3DS/redirects\n- `shopperReference` (string) -- unique ID for the shopper (required for tokenization)\n- `shopperInteraction` (enum) -- `Ecommerce`, `ContAuth`, `Moto`, `POS`\n- `recurringProcessingModel` (enum) -- `CardOnFile`, `Subscription`, `UnscheduledCardOnFile`\n- `storePaymentMethod` (boolean) -- `true` to save payment details\n- `storedPaymentMethodId` (string) -- token ID for charging a saved method\n- `channel` (string) -- `Web`, `iOS`, `Android`\n- `countryCode` (string) -- two-letter country code\n- `additionalData` (object) -- extra data fields\n\n**Response fields:**\n- `resultCode` (string) -- see result codes below\n- `pspReference` (string) -- Adyen unique reference for the payment\n- `action` (object) -- present if redirect/3DS challenge needed\n- `additionalData` (object) -- extra response data\n\n**HTTP responses:** 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 422 Unprocessable Entity, 500 Server Error\n\n#### POST `/sessions` -- Create a payment session (simplified flow)\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `amount` (object, required)\n- `reference` (string, required)\n- `returnUrl` (string, required)\n- `shopperReference` (string)\n- `storePaymentMethod` (boolean)\n- `recurringProcessingModel` (string)\n- `expiresAt` (string) -- session expiry time\n- `countryCode` (string)\n\n**Response fields:**\n- `id` (string) -- session ID\n- `sessionData` (string) -- encrypted session data for client-side\n- Additional payment configuration\n\n#### POST `/paymentMethods` -- List available payment methods\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `amount` (object)\n- `countryCode` (string)\n- `shopperLocale` (string)\n- `shopperReference` (string) -- include to get stored payment methods\n\n**Response fields:**\n- `paymentMethods` (array) -- available payment methods\n- `storedPaymentMethods` (array) -- saved payment methods for the shopper\n\n#### POST `/payments/details` -- Submit additional payment details (3DS, redirects)\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `details` (object) -- details from the redirect/challenge\n- `paymentData` (string) -- from the original payment response action\n\n**Response fields:**\n- `resultCode` (string)\n- `pspReference` (string)\n- `action` (object) -- if further action needed\n- `additionalData` (object)\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/captures` -- Capture an authorized payment\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `amount` (object, required) -- can be partial\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `reference` (string) -- your capture reference\n\n**Response (201):**\n- `pspReference` (string) -- capture operation reference\n- `paymentPspReference` (string) -- original payment reference\n- `status` (string) -- `\"received\"`\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/cancels` -- Cancel an authorized payment\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `reference` (string)\n\n**Response (201):**\n- `pspReference` (string)\n- `paymentPspReference` (string)\n- `status` (string) -- `\"received\"`\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/refunds` -- Refund a captured payment\n\n**Request fields:**\n- `amount` (object, required) -- can be partial\n- `merchantAccount` (string, required)\n- `reference` (string)\n- `merchantRefundReason` (string) -- `FRAUD`, `CUSTOMER REQUEST`, `RETURN`, `DUPLICATE`, `OTHER`\n\n**Response (201):**\n- `pspReference` (string)\n- `paymentPspReference` (string)\n- `status` (string) -- `\"received\"`\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/reversals` -- Reversal (auto cancel or refund)\n\nAutomatically determines whether to cancel (if uncaptured) or refund (if captured). Full amount only.\n\n**Response (201):**\n- `pspReference` (string)\n- `paymentPspReference` (string)\n- `status` (string) -- `\"received\"`\n\n#### POST `/payments/{paymentPspReference}/amountUpdates` -- Adjust authorized amount\n\n#### POST `/cancels` -- Cancel by merchant reference (within 24h, no PSP reference needed)\n\n---\n\n### 4. Customer/Shopper Management\n\nAdyen does NOT have a separate \"Customer\" resource like Stripe. Instead, shoppers are identified by `shopperReference`:\n\n- **shopperReference**: Your unique identifier for the shopper (minimum 3 characters, no PII like email/name)\n- Used consistently across all requests to link payments, tokens, and stored methods to a shopper\n- Token Groups feature (on request) can share references across multiple merchant accounts\n\n**Shopper fields in payment requests:**\n- `shopperReference` (string) -- your unique shopper ID\n- `shopperEmail` (string)\n- `shopperName` (object) -- `{ firstName, lastName }`\n- `shopperIP` (string)\n- `billingAddress` (object)\n- `deliveryAddress` (object)\n- `dateOfBirth` (string)\n- `telephoneNumber` (string)\n- `shopperLocale` (string)\n\n---\n\n### 5. Primary event for payment outcomes. |\n| `CAPTURE` | Capture request result. Not sent for automatic captures by default. |\n| `CAPTURE_FAILED` | Capture rejected by card scheme (rare). |\n| `CANCELLATION` | Cancellation request result. |\n| `CANCEL_OR_REFUND` | Result of cancel-or-refund (reversal) request. |\n| `REFUND` | Refund validation succeeded; sent to card scheme. |\n| `REFUND_FAILED` | Refund rejected by card scheme. |\n| `REFUNDED_REVERSED` | Refunded amount returned to merchant (e.g. invalid bank account). |\n| `REFUND_WITH_DATA` | Outcome of unreferenced refund. |\n| `EXPIRE` | Payment expired on Adyen platform. |\n| `HANDLED_EXTERNALLY` | Payment handled outside Adyen. |\n| `ORDER_OPENED` | First partial payment created an order. |\n| `ORDER_CLOSED` | Final outcome for partial payment orders. |\n| `VOID_PENDING_REFUND` | Cancellation of unreferenced POS refund. |\n| `RECURRING_CONTRACT` | Recurring contract/token created (non-default, must enable). |\n\n#### Dispute Event Codes\n\n| Event Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `CHARGEBACK` | Funds deducted due to chargeback. |\n| `CHARGEBACK_REVERSED` | Chargeback defense initially successful (not final). |\n| `NOTIFICATION_OF_CHARGEBACK` | Dispute opened; investigate and respond. |\n| `NOTIFICATION_OF_FRAUD` | Issuer fraud alert (Visa TC40/Mastercard SAFE). |\n| `REQUEST_FOR_INFORMATION` | Customer opened RFI case. |\n| `SECOND_CHARGEBACK` | Original chargeback defense declined. |\n| `PREARBITRATION_LOST` / `PREARBITRATION_WON` | Pre-arbitration outcomes. |\n\n#### Token Lifecycle Webhook Events\n\n| Event | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `recurring.token.created` | New token created. Contains `data.storedPaymentMethodId`. |\n| `recurring.token.updated` | Token details modified. |\n| `recurring.token.disabled` | Token removed or disabled. |\n| `recurring.token.alreadyExisting` | Duplicate token attempt. |\n\n#### Standard Webhook Payload Structure\n\n```json\n{\n  \"live\": \"false\",\n  \"notificationItems\": [\n    {\n      \"NotificationRequestItem\": {\n        \"eventCode\": \"AUTHORISATION\",\n        \"success\": \"true\",\n        \"pspReference\": \"7914073381342284\",\n        \"originalReference\": \"\",\n        \"merchantAccountCode\": \"YOUR_MERCHANT_ACCOUNT\",\n        \"merchantReference\": \"YOUR_PAYMENT_REFERENCE\",\n        \"amount\": {\n          \"currency\": \"EUR\",\n          \"value\": 1130\n        },\n        \"eventDate\": \"2024-01-15T10:30:00+01:00\",\n        \"operations\": [\"CANCEL\", \"CAPTURE\", \"REFUND\"],\n        \"paymentMethod\": \"visa\",\n        \"reason\": \"\",\n        \"additionalData\": {\n          \"hmacSignature\": \"BASE64_ENCODED_SIGNATURE\"\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n**Required response**: HTTP 200 with body `[accepted]`\n\n#### HMAC Signature Verification\n\n**Algorithm**: HMAC-SHA256\n\n**Fields concatenated in order** (colon-delimited):\n1. `pspReference`\n2. `originalReference`\n3. `merchantAccountCode`\n4. `merchantReference`\n5. `amount.value`\n6. `amount.currency`\n7. `eventCode`\n8. `success`\n\n**Example concatenation**:\n```\n7914073381342284::TestMerchant:TestPayment-1407325143704:1130:EUR:AUTHORISATION:true\n```\n\n**Calculation steps**:\n1. Concatenate fields with `:` delimiter (empty string for missing fields)\n2. Convert payload string to binary (UTF-8)\n3. Convert HMAC key from hex string to binary\n4. Compute HMAC-SHA256(binary_key, binary_payload)\n5. Base64-encode the result\n6. Compare with `additionalData.hmacSignature`\n\n**HMAC key**: Hex string generated in Customer Area (e.g. `44782DEF547AAA06C910C43932B1EB0C71FC68D9D0C057550C48EC2ACF6BA056`)\n\n**Node.js reference implementation**: [adyen-node-api-library hmacValidator.ts](https://github.com/Adyen/adyen-node-api-library/blob/7e76d1b4c87d334f98a1ddc70b4e30a225244d2e/src/utils/hmacValidator.ts)\n\n---\n\n### 8. Payment Result Codes (Complete List)\n\n#### Final States\n\n| Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `Authorised` | Payment successfully authorized. Proceed with delivery. |\n| `Refused` | Payment refused by issuer/acquirer. Check `refusalReason` and `refusalReasonCode`. |\n| `Cancelled` | Payment cancelled by shopper or merchant. |\n| `Error` | Error during processing. |\n\n#### Intermediate States\n\n| Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `Received` | Payment received by Adyen, will be processed (async methods like SEPA). |\n| `Pending` | Payment received, final status not yet available. |\n| `PresentToShopper` | Additional info must be shown to shopper (vouchers, QR codes). |\n\n#### Action-Required States (3DS / Redirects)\n\n| Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `RedirectShopper` | Shopper must be redirected to external page (3DS1, bank redirect). |\n| `ChallengeShopper` | 3DS2 challenge required from issuer. |\n| `IdentifyShopper` | 3DS2 device fingerprinting required. |\n\n#### Special States\n\n| Code | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `AuthenticationFinished` | 3DS2 authentication completed (authentication-only flow). |\n| `AuthenticationNotRequired` | 3DS not required for this transaction. |\n| `PartiallyAuthorised` | Only part of the amount was authorized. |\n\n#### Refusal Reason Codes (when resultCode = Refused)\n\nKey codes: `2` (Refused), `5` (Blocked Card), `6` (Expired Card), `8` (Invalid Card Number), `12` (Not Enough Balance), `14` (Acquirer Fraud), `20` (FRAUD), `24` (CVC Declined), `31` (Issuer Suspected Fraud), `38` (Authentication Required -- retry with 3DS).\n\n---\n\n### 9. Idempotency\n\nSupported via `Idempotency-Key` header on requests. Use a unique key per operation to safely retry failed requests.\n\n---\n\n### 10. Key Integration Notes\n\n- **Amount format**: Always in minor units (cents). EUR 10.00 = `{ currency: \"EUR\", value: 1000 }`\n- **Captures, cancels, refunds are asynchronous**: The API returns `status: \"received\"` immediately. The actual outcome arrives via webhook.\n- **Rely on webhooks, not API responses**: Adyen explicitly recommends relying on the `AUTHORISATION` webhook rather than the `/payments` response for the definitive payment result.\n- **Automatic vs manual capture**: Default is automatic (immediate capture). Can be set to manual or delayed in Customer Area or per-request.\n- **Partial captures**: Supported; unclaimed amount is auto-cancelled (single) or held (multiple, requires enablement).\n- **Partial refunds**: Supported; cumulative total must not exceed captured amount.\n- **No separate Customer API**: Use `shopperReference` consistently across all calls. No need to \"create\" a customer first."}],"totalDurationMs":407958,"totalTokens":38291,"totalToolUseCount":37,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":517,"cache_read_input_tokens":32901,"output_tokens":4872,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":517},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":4872,"cache_read_input_tokens":32901,"cache_creation_input_tokens":517,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":517,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"toolStats":{"readCount":0,"searchCount":0,"bashCount":4,"editFileCount":0,"linesAdded":0,"linesRemoved":0,"otherToolCount":33}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"bf2d7cf8-6849-4709-aba4-502c304cd0a4","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/danielcabral/root-repos/cm/test_collection_module","sessionId":"0882c166-69dc-427f-976d-54dc4572c1e6","version":"2.1.100","gitBranch":"main","slug":"warm-greeting-harbor"}

* Simple Dependency Injection Container\n *\n * Provides a lightweight DI system for managing service dependencies\n * without the complexity of a full-featured DI framework.\n *\n * Features:\n * - Service registration with factory functions\n * - Singleton and transient service lifetimes\n * - Constructor injection pattern\n * - Easy testing with service replacement\n */\n\nexport enum ServiceLifetime {\n  SINGLETON = 'singleton', // One instance for the lifetime of the container\n  TRANSIENT = 'transient', // New instance every time\n}\n\nexport interface ServiceDescriptor<T = any> {\n  token: string | symbol;\n  factory: (container: Container) => T;\n  lifetime: ServiceLifetime;\n  instance?: T; // Cached instance for singletons\n}\n\nexport class Container {\n  private services: Map<string | symbol, ServiceDescriptor> = new Map();\n\n  /**\n   * Register a service with the container\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```typescript\n   * container.register(\n   *   'LogService',\n   *   (container) => new LogService({ environment: 'production' }),\n   *   ServiceLifetime.SINGLETON\n   * );\n   * ```\n   */\n  public register<T>(\n    token: string | symbol,\n    factory: (container: Container) => T,\n    lifetime: ServiceLifetime = ServiceLifetime.SINGLETON\n  ): void {\n    this.services.set(token, {\n      token,\n      factory,\n      lifetime,\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Resolve a service from the container\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```typescript\n   * const logService = container.resolve<LogService>('LogService');\n   * ```\n   */\n  public resolve<T>(token: string | symbol): T {\n    const descriptor = this.services.get(token);\n\n    if (!descriptor) {\n      throw new Error(`Service not registered: ${String(token)}`);\n    }\n\n    // Return singleton instance if it exists\n    if (\n      descriptor.lifetime === ServiceLifetime.SINGLETON &&\n      descriptor.instance\n    ) {\n      return descriptor.instance as T;\n    }\n\n instance;\n    }\n\n    return instance as T;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Check if a service is registered\n   */\n  public has(token: string | symbol): boolean {\n    return this.services.has(token);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Unregister a service (useful for testing)\n   */\n  public unregister(token: string | symbol): void {\n    this.services.delete(token);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Replace a service (useful for testing/mocking)\n   */\n  public replace<T>(\n    token: string | symbol,\n    factory: (container: Container) => T,\n    lifetime: ServiceLifetime = ServiceLifetime.SINGLETON\n  ): void {\n    this.unregister(token);\n    this.register(token, factory, lifetime);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Clear all services\n   */\n  public clear(): void {\n    this.services.clear();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get all ROOT_SERVICE: Symbol('RootService'),\n  PROVIDER_SERVICE: Symbol('ProviderService'),\n  RENDER_SERVICE: Symbol('RenderService'),\n\n  // Add your controller tokens here:\n  // EXAMPLE_CONTROLLER: error: any) => void | Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Retry an async operation with exponential backoff\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const result = await retryWithBackoff(\n *   () => apiClient.call(),\n *   {\n *     maxRetries: 3,\n *     initialDelay: 1000,\n *     shouldRetry: (error) => error.statusCode >= 500\n *   }\n * );\n * ```\n */\nexport async function retryWithBackoff<T>(\n  operation: () => Promise<T>,\n  options: RetryOptions = {}\n): Promise<T> {\n  const {\n    maxRetries = 3,\n    initialDelay = 1000,\n    maxDelay = 30000,\n    backoffMultiplier = 2,\n    shouldRetry = () => true,\n    onRetry,\n  } = options;\n\n  let lastError: Error;\n  let delay = initialDelay;\n\n  for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) environment?: string;\n  skipValidation?: boolean; // For testing\n}\n\nexport enum Environment {\n  PRODUCTION = 'production',\n  SANDBOX = 'sandbox',\n}\n\n/**\n * ConfigurationService - Manages  * Build configuration map from environment variables\n   *\n   * Maps PROVIDER_* env vars to generic providerX config fields.\n   * When switching providers, update env.ts with the new provider's env.ROOT_COLLECTION_MODULE_KEY,\n      environment: this.environment,\n    };\n\n    const production: EnvironmentConfig = {\n      ...baseConfig,\n      environment: Environment.PRODUCTION,\n providerProductId: env.PROVIDER_PRODUCT_ID_LIVE,\n      rootApiKey: env.ROOT_API_KEY_LIVE,\n      rootBaseUrl: env.ROOT_BASE_URL_LIVE,\n    };\n\n    const sandbox: EnvironmentConfig = {\n rootBaseUrl: env.ROOT_BASE_URL_SANDBOX,\n    };\n\n    return {\n      production,\n      sandbox,\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Validate that environment is set correctly and all config values are present\n   */\n  private validateEnvironment(): void {\n    if (!this.environment) {\n      throw new Error(\n        'ENVIRONMENT is not set. Set ENVIRONMENT=production or ENVIRONMENT=sandbox'\n      );\n    }\n\n    const validEnvironments = Object.keys(this.configs);\n\n    if (!validEnvironments.includes(this.environment)) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `Invalid ENVIRONMENT: ${\n          this.environment\n        }. Valid values: ${validEnvironments.join(', ')}`\n      );\n    }\n\n    const config = this.configs[this.environment as keyof ConfigMap];\n    const missingKeys: string[] = [];\n    const invalidUrls: string[] = [];\n\n    // Check for missing values\n    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(config)) {\n      if (value === '' || value === null || value === undefined) {\n        missingKeys.push(key);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Validate URL formats\n    if (config.rootBaseUrl && !this.isValidUrl(config.rootBaseUrl)) {\n      invalidUrls.push(`rootBaseUrl: configuration values for ${\n          this.environment\n        }: ${missingKeys.join(', ')}`,\n        'Hint: Check that code/env.ts is properly configured with all required values.',\n        'For ')}`);\n    }\n\n    if (errors.length > 0) {\n      throw new Error(`Configuration validation failed:\\n${errors.join('\\n')}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Validate URL format\n   */\n  private API Endpoints Needed\n\n| Operation | HTTP Method | Endpoint | Notes |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Create payment (with tokenization) | POST | /payments | Use shopperReference + storePaymentMethod for \"customer creation\" |\n| Create payment (recurring) | POST | /payments | Use storedPaymentMethodId for merchant-initiated payments |\n| List stored payment methods | GET | /storedPaymentMethods | Query by merchantAccount + shopperReference |\n| Delete stored payment method | DELETE | /storedPaymentMethods/{id} | Remove token |\n| Cancel payment | POST | /payments/{pspReference}/cancels | Cancel authorized payment |\n| Refund payment | POST | /payments/{pspReference}/refunds | Refund captured payment |\n\n## 4. Webhook Configuration\n\n```\nWebhook delivery method:   HTTP POST\nSignature header:          (in additionalData.hmacSignature inside payload)\nSignature algorithm:       HMAC-SHA256\nSignature secret config:   providerWebhookSigningSecret\nRequired response:         HTTP 200 with body \"[accepted]\"\n```\n\n### Webhook Events to Handle\n\n| Event name | Trigger | Root Platform action |\n|---|---|---|\n| AUTHORISATION (success=true) | Payment authorized | Mark Root payment as successful |\n| AUTHORISATION (success=false) | Payment refused | Mark Root payment as failed |\n| CAPTURE | Payment captured | (informational) |\n| CANCELLATION | Payment cancelled | Mark Root payment as cancelled |\n| REFUND | Refund processed | Mark Root payment as refunded |\n| REFUND_FAILED | Refund failed | Log warning |\n| CHARGEBACK | Chargeback received | Mark Root payment as failed |\n\n## 5. Data Shapes\n\n### Shopper reference (Adyen has no Customer object)\n```json\n{\n  \"shopperReference\": \"root_policy_123\",\n  \"shopperEmail\": \"customer@example.com\",\n  \"shopperName\": { \"firstName\": \"Jane\", \"lastName\": \"Doe\" }\n}\n```\n\n### Payment response\n```json\n{\n  \"pspReference\": \"7914073381342284\",\n  \"resultCode\": \"Authorised\",\n  \"amount\": { \"currency\": \"ZAR\", \"value\": 50000 },\n  \"merchantReference\": \"payment_test_123\"\n}\n```\n\n### Webhook notification item\n```json\n{\n  \"NotificationRequestItem\": {\n    \"eventCode\": \"AUTHORISATION\",\n    \"success\": \"true\",\n    \"pspReference\": \"7914073381342284\",\n    \"merchantReference\": \"payment_test_123\",\n    \"amount\": { \"currency\": \"ZAR\", \"value\": 50000 },\n    \"additionalData\": { \"hmacSignature\": \"...\" }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Payment status mapping\n\n| Provider resultCode | Root Platform status |\n|---|---|\n| Authorised | successful |\n| Received | pending |\n| Pending | pending |\n| Refused | failed |\n| Cancelled | cancelled |\n| Error | failed |\n\n## 6. Configuration Fields\n\n| Config field | Description | Example value |\n|---|---|---|\n| `providerSecretKey` | Adyen API key | `AQE...` |\n| `providerWebhookSigningSecret` | HMAC key (hex string) | `44782DEF547...` |\n| `providerMerchantAccount` | Merchant account name | `YourCompanyECOM` |\n| `providerPublishableKey` | Client key (for Drop-in) | `test_xxx` |\n\n## 7. Lifecycle Hook Behaviour\n\n| Hook | Action |\n|---|---|\n| `afterPolicyIssued` | Generate shopperReference from policy, store in app_data (no API call needed) |\n| `afterPolicyPaymentMethodAssigned` | Store storedPaymentMethodId in app_data |\n| `afterPaymentCreated` | POST /payments with storedPaymentMethodId for recurring charge |\n| `afterPolicyCancelled` | DELETE /storedPaymentMethods/{id} to disable token |\n\n## 8. Error Scenarios\n\n| Scenario | Provider error | Handling |\n|---|---|---|\n| Payment refused | resultCode: \"Refused\" | Mark payment failed with refusalReason |\n| Invalid API key | HTTP 401 | Throw ModuleError |\n| Webhook HMAC invalid | Signature mismatch | Return 403, log warning |\n| Rate limited | HTTP 429 | Retry with backoff |\n\n## 9. Additional Notes\n\n- Amounts are always in minor units (cents). ZAR 500.00 = `{ currency: \"ZAR\", value: 50000 }`\n- Captures, cancels, refunds are asynchronous — API returns `status: \"received\"`, outcome arrives via webhook\n- Adyen has no separate Customer API — use `shopperReference` consistently across all requests\n- Base URLs differ per environment:\n  - Test: `https://checkout-test.adyen.com/v71`\n  - Live: `https://{prefix}-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout/v71`\n- Idempotency supported via `Idempotency-Key` header\n- HMAC verification concatenates: pspReference:originalReference:merchantAccountCode:merchantReference:amount.value:amount.currency:eventCode:success\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":754,"cache_read_input_tokens":55215,"output_tokens":1560,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":754,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1560,"cache_read_input_tokens":55215,"cache_creation_input_tokens":754,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":754},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZuuABVawN4F2xofBbk2k","type":"assistant","uuid":"fbcadd02-c3dd-493e-a16c-7d6f80c80cc8","timestamp":"2026-04-10T10:04:07.175Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/danielcabral/root-repos/cm/test_collection_module","sessionId":"0882c166-69dc-427f-976d-54dc4572c1e6","version":"2.1.100","gitBranch":"main","slug":"warm-greeting-harbor"}

: 'https://checkout-test.adyen.com/v71';\n\n    this.merchantAccount = config.get('providerMerchantAccount');\n\n    this.sdk = new BaseHttpClient({\n      baseUrl,\n      apiKey: naming (provider*) so the same\n * service works regardless of which payment provider is integrated.\n */\n\nimport * as env from '../env';\n\nexport interface EnvironmentConfig {\n  timeDelayInMilliseconds: string;\n  environment: Environment;\n  rootCollectionModuleKey: string;\n  rootApiKey: string;\n  rootBaseUrl: string;\n  // Provider-specific config — use generic names that work for any provider.\n  providerWebhookSigningSecret: string;\n  providerPublishableKey: string;\n  providerSecretKey: string;\n  providerProductId: string;\n}\n\nexport interface ConfigMap {\n  production: EnvironmentConfig;\n  sandbox: EnvironmentConfig;\n}\n\nexport interface ConfigurationServiceOptions {\n  environment?: string;\n  skipValidation?: boolean; // For testing\n}\n\nexport enum Environment {\n  PRODUCTION = 'production',\n  SANDBOX = 'sandbox',\n}\n\n/**\n * ConfigurationService - Manages environment-specific configuration\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const config = new ConfigurationService();\n * const apiKey = config.get('providerSecretKey');\n * const isProduction = config.isProduction();\n * ```\n */\nexport class ConfigurationService {\n  private readonly configs: ConfigMap;\n  private readonly environment: string;\n  private readonly currentConfig: EnvironmentConfig;\n\n  constructor(options: ConfigurationServiceOptions = {}) {\n    this.environment =\n      options.environment || process.env.ENVIRONMENT || Environment.SANDBOX;\n    this.configs = this.buildConfigMap();\n\n    if (!options.skipValidation) {\n      this.validateEnvironment();\n    }\n\n    this.currentConfig = this.configs[this.environment as keyof ConfigMap];\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Build configuration map from environment variables\n   *\n   * Maps PROVIDER_* env vars to generic providerX config fields.\n   * When switching providers, update env.ts with the new provider's values.\n   */\n  private buildConfigMap(): ConfigMap {\n    const const production: EnvironmentConfig = {\n      ...baseConfig,\n      environment: Environment.PRODUCTION,\n      providerWebhookSigningSecret: env.PROVIDER_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET_LIVE,\n      providerPublishableKey: env.PROVIDER_PUBLISHABLE_KEY_LIVE,\n      providerSecretKey: env.PROVIDER_SECRET_KEY_LIVE,\n      providerProductId: env.PROVIDER_PRODUCT_ID_LIVE,\n      rootApiKey: env.ROOT_API_KEY_LIVE,\n      rootBaseUrl: env.ROOT_BASE_URL_LIVE,\n    };\n\n    const sandbox: EnvironmentConfig = {\n      ...baseConfig,\n      environment: Environment.SANDBOX,\n      providerWebhookSigningSecret: env.PROVIDER_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET_TEST,\n      providerPublishableKey: env.PROVIDER_PUBLISHABLE_KEY_TEST,\n      providerSecretKey: env.PROVIDER_SECRET_KEY_TEST,\n      providerProductId: env.PROVIDER_PRODUCT_ID_TEST,\n      rootApiKey: env.ROOT_API_KEY_SANDBOX,\n      rootBaseUrl: env.ROOT_BASE_URL_SANDBOX,\n    };\n\n    return {\n      production,\n      sandbox,\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Validate that environment is set correctly and all config values are present\n   */\n  private validateEnvironment(): void {\n    if (!this.environment) {\n      throw new Error(\n        'ENVIRONMENT is not set. Set ENVIRONMENT=production or ENVIRONMENT=sandbox'\n      );\n    }\n\n    const validEnvironments = Object.keys(this.configs);\n\n    if (!validEnvironments.includes(this.environment)) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `Invalid ENVIRONMENT: ${\n          this.environment\n        }. Valid values: ${validEnvironments.join(', ')}`\n      );\n    }\n\n    const config = this.configs[this.environment as keyof ConfigMap];\n    const missingKeys: string[] = [];\n    const invalidUrls: string[] = [];\n\n    // Check for missing values\n    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(config)) {\n      if (value === '' || value === null || value === undefined) {\n        missingKeys.push(key);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Validate URL formats\n    if (config.rootBaseUrl && !this.isValidUrl(config.rootBaseUrl)) {\n      invalidUrls.push(`rootBaseUrl: \"${config.rootBaseUrl}\"`);\n    }\n\n    // Combine validation errors\n    const errors: string[] = [];\n\n    if (missingKeys.length > 0) {\n      errors.push(\n        `Missing required configuration values for ${\n          this.environment\n        }: ${missingKeys.join(', ')}`,\n        'Hint: Check that code/env.ts is properly configured with all required values.',\n        'For AWS Lambda: Ensure all environment variables are set in Lambda configuration.'\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (invalidUrls.length > 0) {\n      errors.push(`Invalid URL format(s): ${invalidUrls.join(', ')}`);\n    }\n\n    if (errors.length > 0) {\n      throw new Error(`Configuration validation failed:\\n${errors.join('\\n')}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Validate URL format\n   */\n  private isValidUrl(url: string): boolean {\n    try {\n      const parsed = new URL(url);\n      return ['http:', 'https:'].includes(parsed.protocol);\n    } catch {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get a configuration value by key\n   */\n  public get<K extends keyof EnvironmentConfig>(key: K): EnvironmentConfig[K] {\n    return this.currentConfig[key];\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get the getEnvironment(): string {\n    return this.environment;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Check if running in production\n   */\n  public isProduction(): boolean {\n    return this.environment === Environment.PRODUCTION.toString();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Check if running in sandbox\n   */\n  public isSandbox(): boolean {\n    return this.environment === Environment.SANDBOX.toString();\n  }\n\n type-safe access to environment-specific configuration.\n * It validates configuration on initialization and throws errors for missing values.\n *\n * Config field names use provider-agnostic naming (provider*) so the same\n * service works regardless of which payment provider is integrated.\n */\n\nimport * as env from '../env';\n\nexport interface EnvironmentConfig {\n  timeDelayInMilliseconds: string;\n  environment: Environment;\n  rootCollectionModuleKey: string;\n  rootApiKey: string;\n  rootBaseUrl: string;\n  // Provider-specific config — use generic names that work for any provider.\n  providerWebhookSigningSecret: string;\n  providerPublishableKey: string;\n  providerSecretKey: string;\n  providerProductId: string;\n  providerMerchantAccount: string;\n}\n\nexport interface ConfigMap {\n  production: EnvironmentConfig;\n  sandbox: EnvironmentConfig;\n}\n\nexport interface ConfigurationServiceOptions {\n  environment?: string;\n  skipValidation?: boolean; // For testing\n}\n\nexport enum Environment {\n  PRODUCTION = 'production',\n  SANDBOX = 'sandbox',\n}\n\n/**\n * ConfigurationService - Manages environment-specific configuration\n *\n * @example\n * ConfigurationService {\n  private readonly configs: ConfigMap;\n  private readonly environment: string;\n  private readonly currentConfig: EnvironmentConfig;\n\n  constructor(options: (!options.skipValidation) {\n      this.validateEnvironment();\n    }\n\n    this.currentConfig = this.configs[this.environment as keyof ConfigMap];\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Build configuration map from void {\n    if (!this.environment) {\n      throw new Error(\n        'ENVIRONMENT is not set. Set ENVIRONMENT=production or ENVIRONMENT=sandbox'\n      );\n    }\n\n    const validEnvironments = Object.keys(this.configs);\n\n    if (!validEnvironments.includes(this.environment)) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `Invalid ENVIRONMENT: ${\n          this.environment\n        }. Valid values: ${validEnvironments.join(', ')}`\n      );\n    }\n\n    const config = this.configs[this.environment as keyof ConfigMap];\n    const missingKeys: string[] = [];\n    const invalidUrls: string[] = [];\n\n    // Check for missing values\n    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(config)) {\n      if (value === '' || value === null || value === undefined) {\n configuration values for ${\n          this.environment\n        }: ${missingKeys.join(', ')}`,\n        'Hint: Check that code/env.ts is properly configured with all required values.',\n        'For AWS Lambda: Ensure all environment variables are set in Lambda configuration.'\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (invalidUrls.length > 0) {\n      errors.push(`Invalid URL format(s): ${invalidUrls.join(', ')}`);\n    }\n\n    if (errors.length > 0) {\n      throw new Error(`Configuration validation failed:\\n${errors.join('\\n')}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Validate URL format\n   */\n  private isValidUrl(url: string): boolean {\n    try {\n      const parsed = new URL(url);\n      return ['http:', 'https:'].includes(parsed.protocol);\n    } catch {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get a configuration value by key\n   */\n  public get<K extends keyof EnvironmentConfig>(key: K): EnvironmentConfig[K] {\n    return this.currentConfig[key];\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get the
